mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 10.0% signals severe labor market distress and collapsing consumer income, which forces equity markets to price in recessionary earnings cuts, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in 60% of instances when UNRATE crosses 10.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise, or the interaction term shows negative predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect rapid supply-side normalization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat following INDPRO spikes during recovery phases, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.5% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 55% of cases when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.5% monthly threshold, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending forecasts and drives positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal predicts negative returns in 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when tested on out-of-sample data.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal forward-looking consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into positive corporate earnings revisions and positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST increases over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 consistently.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signal supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in 60% of instances when INDPRO falls during late-cycle phases, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance in regime-filtered backtests.
